item.title: Chinese repression in Switzerland: NGOs criticize Bern
item.description: NGOs denounce the lack of concrete measures to protect Tibetans and Uyghurs in Switzerland despite recognizing transnational repression
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- NGOs disappointed by lack of concrete actions against Chinese repression
- Report by the Federal Council of February 12, 2025
- 1,375 documented cases of physical repression since 2014

## Key facts
- **What**: Transnational repression against Tibetans and Uyghurs
- When: For years, with official recognition in 2025
- Where: Switzerland, with documented cases internationally
- Who: NGO Voices, Uyghur Association of Switzerland, Young Tibetans in Europe Association
- Amount: 1,375 documented physical cases since 2014

The Swiss Federal Council recognized in 2025 the surveillance and pressure activities carried out by the Chinese government on Tibetan and Uyghur communities in Switzerland. However, NGOs criticize the lack of concrete measures to protect these communities. Selina Morell, head of the China program at Voices, states: 'We are very disappointed that, concretely, nothing has changed.'

Forms of repression include physical and virtual intimidation, direct or indirect through family members, with the aim of silencing dissenting voices abroad. There are no precise figures on repression in Switzerland, but observers believe the phenomenon is increasing. Internationally, the NGO Freedom House has documented 1,375 cases since 2014, but these are only the most serious physical episodes.

### Measures announced but not yet implemented

In the report published on February 12, 2025, the Federal Council outlined several measures, including bilateral dialogue with Beijing, raising awareness among actors potentially confronted with the phenomenon, exchanging with the affected diasporas, and creating a consultation service for victims. However, no timeline has been set for the implementation of these measures.

The Federal Department of Justice and Police (DFJP) indicates that work on national measures has begun, with the establishment of a strategic steering group active since February. This group will produce by the end of the year a mapping of competencies in transnational repression at the municipal, cantonal, and federal levels. Next year, the group will focus on analyzing communication channels with the affected diasporas and the areas of awareness and prevention.

Selina Morell welcomes the creation of the group but deplores the slowness of the process. 'The pressure continues,' says Rizwana Ilham, president of the Uyghur Association of Switzerland. 'The publication of the report was a relief, because it shows that what we have been denouncing for years is real. But in practice, almost nothing has changed.'

### Direct testimonies

Rizwana Ilham, originally from Urumqi, recounts having been subjected to attempts at intimidation. 'At every demonstration, people from China follow us, photograph us, ask who the organizer is, without fearing consequences. It is shocking that this happens in Switzerland,' she says. Arya Amipa, co-president of the Young Tibetans in Europe Association, recounts being the target of anonymous intimidating phone calls and several attempts at cyber piracy.

### Deterrent effect

'These actions are coordinated by China, sometimes with the complicity, in Switzerland, of diplomatic personnel or members of the diaspora recruited by the Chinese authorities,' explains Selina Morell. The sad reality is that this system works. Fewer and fewer people participate in demonstrations. Some young people tell me they no longer dare to come, because they fear for their relatives in Tibet. It is a real risk: the families of some demonstrators have received visits from Chinese police, who have threatened them to put pressure on their relatives in Switzerland,' says Arya Amipa.

### NGOs' priorities

According to Voices and the diaspora associations, the priority should be the creation of a contact point for victims, which would provide support and protection. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The transnational repression does not only concern the Tibetan and Uyghur communities, but it has broader implications for all cross-border workers living and working in Switzerland. The lack of concrete measures to protect the victims of this repression can create a climate of insecurity and fear, influencing the daily life of many.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the official recognition of transnational repression by the Federal Council, the Tibetan and Uyghur communities in Switzerland had been denouncing for years the pressures and intimidations they suffered. However, without concrete measures, the situation remains critical. The creation of a strategic steering group is a step forward, but NGOs believe that it is necessary to act with greater urgency.

### Possible scenarios

If the Swiss government does not take concrete measures to protect the victims of transnational repression, the phenomenon could continue to grow, with negative consequences for security and freedom of expression in Switzerland. On the other hand, the implementation of effective measures could contribute to creating a safer environment for all communities, including cross-border workers.

### International comparisons

Transnational repression is not an isolated phenomenon. Other countries, such as Canada and the United States, have adopted measures to protect communities victimized by pressures from foreign governments. Switzerland could draw inspiration from these experiences to develop effective strategies.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do if you are a victim of transnational repression

If you are a victim of transnational repression, it is important to seek support and protection. NGOs like Voices offer advice and assistance. It is crucial to document each episode of intimidation and report dangerous situations to the competent authorities.

### Procedure to report episodes of repression

1. **Document**: Record each episode of intimidation, including dates, times, and details of the people involved.
2. Contact NGOs: Turn to organizations like Voices to obtain support and advice.
3. Report to the authorities: File a complaint with the competent Swiss authorities, providing all the evidence collected.
4. Seek protection: Request protective measures if you are in immediate danger.

### Useful tools

For further information and support, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate your income and plan your expenses. Additionally, the [health insurance comparator](nav:health) can help you find the coverage best suited to your needs.

### Conclusion

Transnational repression is a complex phenomenon that requires the attention and action of the Swiss authorities. NGOs continue to demand concrete measures to protect victims and ensure the safety of all communities in Switzerland. If you are a victim of repression, it is essential to seek support and protection.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3699705)*

item.title: A2: heavy traffic and delays over an hour
item.description: Lane reduction causing delays up to 100 minutes between Chiasso and Grancia. Police present for checks.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Single-lane restriction on the A2
- Delays up to 100 minutes northbound
- Heavy traffic between Chiasso and Grancia

## Key facts
- **What**: Single-lane restriction
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Between Chiasso and Grancia
- Who: TCS, Police
- Impact: Delays up to 100 minutes

The reduction to a single lane on the A2 is causing heavy traffic between Chiasso and Grancia. The queue this morning started early, as of 6 AM, right after Mendrisio. TCS confirms disruptions between Chiasso and the Grancia tunnel: congested traffic with delays up to one hour and forty minutes. Before the San Nicolao tunnel, the lanes have been reduced from two to one, causing significant delays. Before the Melide dam bridge, there is at least one police patrol checking the road surface.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers using the A2 northbound should expect longer than usual travel times. Significant delays can affect work schedules and daily activities. It is advisable to leave early to avoid further disruptions.

Police checks could cause additional slowdowns. It is important to respect speed limits and road signs to avoid fines and ensure the safety of all road users.

### Practical tips

For cross-border workers who need to use the A2 highway, it is advisable to:
- Leave early to avoid delays
- Check traffic conditions before departure
- Respect speed limits and road signs
- Keep necessary documents handy for police checks

### Possible scenarios

If maintenance work were to extend over a longer period, cross-border workers might need to consider alternative transportation, such as the train or carpooling. Additionally, temporary measures may be introduced to manage traffic, such as activating preferential lanes for vehicles with more passengers.

### Conclusion

The single-lane restriction on the A2 is causing significant disruptions for cross-border workers using the highway northbound. It is important to be prepared and plan the journey in advance to avoid delays and ensure a productive workday. ## Analysis of causes and solutions

The reduction to one lane on the A2 is due to road maintenance work. These works are necessary to ensure the safety and functionality of the highway, but they cause temporary inconveniences for users. The competent authorities are trying to minimize the inconveniences through constant checks and monitoring.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the maintenance work, the A2 highway had two lanes in both directions, allowing for a smoother flow of traffic. With the reduction to one lane, traffic has increased significantly, causing delays and congestion.

### Future scenarios

If the maintenance work were to drag on for a longer period, the authorities might have to consider introducing temporary measures to manage traffic. These measures could include the activation of preferential lanes for vehicles with more passengers or the introduction of peak hours with access restrictions.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Furthermore, it is crucial to respect the speed limits and road signs to ensure the safety of all road users.

The competent authorities are working to minimize the inconveniences and ensure the safety of all road users.

## Recommended tools

item.title: Grisons tighten permits after mafia infiltration
item.description: New rules for residence permits in Grisons after arrests linked to the Camorra and 'Ndrangheta in Roveredo
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Grisons changes residence permit rules
- Criminal record declaration mandatory
- Stricter controls on the way

## Key facts
- **What**: New rules for residence permits
- When: To be defined
- Where: Canton of Grisons
- Who: State Councillor Peter Peyer
- Amount: Not specified

Following the arrest of four people linked to the Camorra and ’Ndrangheta residing in Roveredo, the Canton of Grisons announced a change in the rules for residence permits. Applicants will have to declare any criminal records or ongoing proceedings. This measure could lead to stricter controls.

Grisons State Councillor Peter Peyer announced that applicants will have to explicitly declare any criminal records or ongoing proceedings. This information could lead to further checks, such as requesting a criminal record extract. In the presence of well-founded doubts, these elements could be considered in the material judgment and motivate a negative decision.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The new procedure could have a significant impact on cross-border workers wishing to obtain a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons. Those who provide false information in the self-declaration will risk the immediate withdrawal of the permit. The exact date for the entry into force of the new procedure has not yet been established.

Grisons parliamentarian Samuele Censi raised the issue in the Grand Council, intervening at the cantonal level in collaboration with the Municipality of Roveredo from the outset. Censi also presented an interpellation signed by the deputies of the Roveredo district to ask, among other things, whether the Canton intends to follow the path already taken by Ticino, introducing the systematic request for a criminal record extract.

### Comparison with Ticino

The change in procedure was welcomed with moderate optimism by Censi, albeit with the awareness that it is not a definitive solution. 'Ticino in 2009 also adopted a similar measure before moving in 2015 to the systematic request for a criminal record. This is an intermediate step. What matters most is that today there is greater awareness of the problem and the willingness to address it,' emphasized the deputy.

In a first phase, admits Censi, 'we perceived a certain lack of sensitivity on the part of the Government. The issue then became of cantonal and national scope, pushing the Executive to intervene. There is satisfaction in noting that our steps have led to concrete results.' However, the need to strengthen the information network remains: 'I believe there is a lack of closer collaboration and systematic exchange of information between the various departments and with other cantons,' concludes Censi.

### Next steps

Institutions are taking action. Thanks also to the intervention of the Moesano deputies, a first meeting is scheduled for mid-May in Roveredo, involving representatives of the Moesano municipalities, employees of the Commercial Register Office, the Public Prosecutor's Office, and the Grisons Cantonal Police. In mid-June, a further summit is also planned between the law enforcement agencies of the Cantons of Grisons and Ticino.

Among the topics of discussion will also be a crucial issue: how to verify the actual operability of companies with legal personality, in order to prevent fictitious companies from entering into employment contracts solely for the purpose of obtaining residence permits for people linked to organized crime. The phenomenon particularly affects Moesano because, as Censi observes, 'Ticino has tightened the screws and the problem has shifted to us.' To counter the so-called 'letterbox companies,' explains the mayor of Grono, 'in collaboration with the Canton, we have introduced an additional verification step: in Moesano, we systematically ask new companies to confirm their address. The company must submit to the Municipality the documents certifying its location.' ### Analysis of the new rules

The new rules for residence permits in the Canton of Grisons represent a significant step towards greater transparency and security. The mandatory declaration of any criminal records or ongoing proceedings is a measure aimed at preventing the infiltration of criminal organizations into the cantonal territory.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who wish to obtain a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons, the new rules involve greater attention in completing the application. It is essential to provide accurate and complete information to avoid the withdrawal of the permit. Requesting a criminal record extract could become a systematic practice, as is already the case in the Canton of Ticino.

### Comparison with the Canton of Ticino

The Canton of Ticino has already adopted similar measures in 2009, then moving in 2015 to the systematic request of the criminal record. This comparison highlights the importance of closer collaboration between the cantons to effectively address the problem of mafia infiltration.

### Verification of companies

The verification of the actual operation of companies is a crucial issue to prevent the use of fictitious companies to obtain residence permits. In Moesano, an additional verification step is being introduced for new companies, requiring confirmation of the address and delivery of documents certifying the location.

### Collaboration between cantons

Collaboration between the law enforcement agencies of the Cantons of Grisons and Ticino is essential to address the problem of mafia infiltration. The meetings scheduled for May and June represent an important step towards greater cooperation and exchange of information.

### Conclusion

The new rules for residence permits in the Canton of Grisons represent a significant step towards greater security and transparency. Collaboration between the cantons and the introduction of stricter controls are fundamental to prevent the infiltration of criminal organizations into the cantonal territory.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to obtain a residence permit

To obtain a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons, it is important to carefully follow the new rules. Here are the steps to follow:

1. **Fill out the application**: Provide all the requested information accurately and completely.
2. Declare any criminal records: Indicate any criminal records or ongoing proceedings.
3. Provide verification documents: Submit documents that certify the location of the company, if applicable.
4. Wait for the response: Wait for the decision of the cantonal authorities.

### Useful tools

For further information and assistance, you can use the [residence permit calculator](nav:permit-compare) available on the site. This tool can help you better understand the new rules and requirements for obtaining a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons.

### Conclusion

The new rules for residence permits in the Canton of Grisons represent an important step towards greater security and transparency. Carefully following the procedures and providing accurate information is fundamental to obtaining a residence permit without problems.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/lavoro-ed-economia/grigioni-stretta-sui-permessi-di-dimora-dopo-linfiltrazione-mafiosa-a-roveredo/91305872)*

item.title: Malpensa: €6m in cash seized in 3 months
item.description: Finance Police and Customs checks reveal violations for over €6 million in undeclared cash.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 6.1 million euros seized in 3 months
- 473 passengers with undeclared cash
- Fines totaling 370,000 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: Checks on passengers with undeclared cash
- When: January-March 2026
- Where: Malpensa Airport
- Who: Guardia di Finanza and Customs Agency
- Amount: 6.1 million euros seized

At Malpensa, over 6 million euros in cash exceeding the allowed threshold were discovered in three months. This is the result of the control activity conducted at Milan Malpensa Airport by the Guardia di Finanza of Varese together with officials from the Customs and Monopoly Agency. Between January and March, checks were carried out on over 700 passengers entering and exiting the national territory. Of these, 473 were found to be in possession of sums exceeding 10,000 euros, the limit beyond which declaration is mandatory. The violations involved both Italian and foreign citizens, often heading to countries such as the Arabian Peninsula, China, Turkey, Senegal, and Egypt.

### Concealment methods
Various tricks were used to hide the money: cash hidden in clothing or luggage, but also more sophisticated forms. In many operations, the nose of Yoltan, a German Shepherd 'cashdog' trained to detect the smell of banknotes, proved decisive, as well as the use of scanners for luggage checks. Among the most peculiar cases was that of an Italian citizen residing abroad, stopped upon departure with 12 US gold coins worth over 18,000 euros.

### Fines and seizures
Overall, the activity led to the administrative seizure of approximately 234,000 euros, collected by the Treasury according to the new regulatory provisions, and the imposition of fines totaling around 370,000 euros. The checks are part of a broader system to combat illegal money flows and continue with further investigations to verify the origin of the intercepted sums.

### The new law
The Legislative Decree No. 211/2024, in force since January 17, 2025, regulates the cross-border transfer of cash, strengthening controls against money laundering and illegal financing. The obligation to declare sums equal to or exceeding 10,000 euros remains, both when entering and exiting Italy, including instruments assimilated to cash such as checks or gold coins. The regulation introduces more streamlined and immediate procedures: in case of violation, the 'immediate oblation' is provided, which allows closing the dispute immediately by paying a reduced fine (15% of the excess up to 10,000 euros, 30% up to 40,000 euros). Beyond these thresholds, higher fines apply. The decree also strengthens the control powers of the Guardia di Finanza and the Customs Agency, which can proceed with the seizure of the exceeding sums and initiate investigations into the origin of the money, within the framework of activities to prevent financial crimes.

### The cash dogs
The 'cash dogs' of the Guardia di Finanza are canine units specialized in detecting cash, trained to recognize the smell of banknotes through a targeted and progressive training path. The selection starts with dogs with outstanding olfactory abilities, behavioral balance, and a predisposition to work, often German or Belgian shepherds. The training is based on the positive reinforcement method: the dog learns to associate the smell of money with a reward, such as play or food. Initially, work is done in controlled environments, then in increasingly complex contexts, simulating real situations such as airports, suitcases, or vehicles. The dogs are trained to signal the presence of cash without damaging it, with passive behaviors such as sitting or stopping. Fundamental is the bond with the handler, who is trained together with the dog to correctly interpret its signals. Training continues even after operational deployment, with constant exercises to maintain the dog's abilities at a high level. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The reinforced checks at Malpensa have a significant impact on cross-border workers who frequently travel between Italy and Switzerland. With the entry into force of the new decree, it is crucial that cross-border workers are aware of the new regulations and the penalties for transferring undeclared cash. The 10,000 euro threshold is a strict limit, and exceeding it without declaration can result in heavy fines.

### Procedures for travelers
To avoid problems, travelers must follow some key procedures:
1. **Mandatory declaration**: If you are carrying amounts exceeding 10,000 euros, you must complete a declaration with the customs authorities.
2. Documentation: Always keep documentation at hand that proves the legitimate origin of the money.
3. Use of financial instruments: Consider using bank transfers or other financial instruments to transfer large sums, thus avoiding the physical transport of cash.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the entry into force of the new decree, the penalties for transporting undeclared cash were less severe. With the new provisions, penalties have been increased and control procedures have become stricter. This change aims to more effectively combat money laundering and illegal financing, thus protecting the legal economy.

### Practical scenarios
Imagine a cross-border worker who regularly travels between Varese and Lugano. If this cross-border worker needs to transfer an amount exceeding 10,000 euros, they must declare it to the customs authorities. Otherwise, they risk incurring heavy fines. It is therefore essential to always be informed and prepared to avoid unpleasant surprises during checks.

### Conclusion
The checks at Malpensa represent a concrete example of the new measures adopted to combat money laundering and illegal financing. For cross-border workers, it is essential to be aware of the new regulations and follow the correct procedures to avoid penalties. The collaboration between the Guardia di Finanza and the Customs Agency is fundamental to ensuring the security and legality of money transfers.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do in case of a check
If you are subjected to a check at Malpensa and you are in possession of cash exceeding 10,000 euros, it is important to follow these procedures:
1. **Immediate declaration**: Immediately inform the customs authorities of the presence of cash exceeding the allowed threshold.
2. Documentation: Provide all necessary documentation to attest to the legitimate origin of the money.
3. Collaboration: Collaborate with the authorities during the check, providing all the information requested.

### Useful tools
To always be updated on regulations and procedures to follow, you can use the [cash calculator](nav:calculator) available on our site. This tool will help you calculate the allowed cash thresholds and better understand the penalties provided.

### Final conclusions
Checks at Malpensa represent a concrete example of the new measures adopted to combat money laundering and illegal financing. For cross-border workers, it is essential to be aware of the new regulations and follow the correct procedures to avoid penalties. Collaboration between the Guardia di Finanza and the Customs Agency is fundamental to ensuring the security and legality of money transfers.

### Final CTA
For more information and to use the cash calculator, visit our site and discover how to protect yourself from penalties and travel safely.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/a-malpensa-in-tre-mesi-scoperti-oltre-6-milioni-di-euro-in-contanti-oltre-la-soglia-consentita-sanzioni-per-370-mila-euro/2561311/)*

item.title: Ispra: success at the second solidarity lunch in the oratory
item.description: Great turnout in Ispra for the solidarity lunch organized by the Pastoral Community and the Association of Ispra Volunteers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- High turnout at the second solidarity lunch in Ispra
- Organized by the Pastoral Community and Ispra Volunteers Association
- Risotto prepared by volunteer chefs

## Key facts
- **What**: Second solidarity lunch
- When: Sunday, April 26, 2026
- Where: Ispra Oratory
- Who: Pastoral Community "Blessed of the Cross" and Ispra Volunteers Association
- Amount: Not specified

The hall of the Ispra Oratory quickly filled up on Sunday, April 26, for the second appointment with the solidarity lunch dedicated to lonely people from Ispra and nearby towns. The initiative, organized by the Pastoral Community "Blessed of the Cross" and the Ispra Volunteers Association, confirmed the strong desire for sharing and closeness among citizens. The hall welcomed numerous people from Ispra and Cadrezzate with Osmate, creating a festive atmosphere, just like a big family. The absolute star of the table was an excellent risotto, carefully prepared by expert volunteer chefs, which warmed not only the palate but also the hearts of the diners. "A moment of celebration to be lived together – say the organizers – to break loneliness and feel part of a big family. A special thanks goes to all the volunteers from Ispra and Cadrezzate with Osmate who, with their commitment, made this wonderful day of solidarity possible."

### The importance of solidarity
Solidarity and sharing are fundamental values that can make a difference in people's lives. Initiatives like the solidarity lunch in Ispra show how the community can come together to support those in need. This type of event not only provides a hot meal but also creates a sense of belonging and mutual support. The active participation of volunteers and the positive response of citizens highlight the importance of keeping solidarity and closeness among people alive.

### The role of volunteers
Volunteers play a crucial role in events like these. Their dedication and commitment are essential to ensure the success of solidarity initiatives. In Ispra, volunteers worked tirelessly to prepare the risotto and welcome participants, creating a welcoming and festive environment. Their contribution was fundamental to transforming a simple lunch into a moment of sharing and solidarity. The organizers' gratitude towards the volunteers underscores the importance of teamwork and collaboration in the community. ### The implications for the community
Events like the solidarity lunch in Ispra have a significant impact on the local community. They not only provide concrete support to lonely individuals but also strengthen social bonds and promote a sense of belonging. The active participation of citizens and volunteers demonstrates how solidarity can be a shared value and practiced daily. These events create an environment where people can feel supported and valued, contributing to improving the quality of life in the community.

### Comparison with other initiatives
The solidarity lunch in Ispra is part of a series of similar initiatives that are gaining traction in many communities. These events are often organized by local associations, parishes, or volunteer groups that want to make a difference in people's lives. Comparing with other similar initiatives shows how solidarity can be promoted in different ways, but with the same goal of supporting those in need. The active participation of citizens and collaboration between different organizations are key elements for the success of these initiatives.

### Future scenarios
The positive response to the solidarity lunch in Ispra paves the way for new opportunities for similar events in the future. The community could organize other solidarity lunches or support initiatives, involving more and more people and promoting solidarity. Collaboration between the Pastoral Community, the Ispra Volunteers Association, and other local groups could lead to broader and more lasting projects, contributing to creating a more cohesive and supportive community. The active participation of citizens and the support of local institutions will be fundamental for the success of these initiatives.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### How to Participate
If you're interested in participating or contributing to solidarity initiatives like the lunch in Ispra, there are several ways to do so. You can contact the "Benedetta della Croce" pastoral community or the Ispra Volunteers Association for information about future events and how to become a volunteer. Your participation can make a difference in people's lives and help strengthen solidarity in the community.

### Procedure to Organize a Similar Event
If you want to organize a solidarity event in your community, there are some fundamental steps to follow. First, identify a group of volunteers willing to collaborate and establish the objectives of the initiative. Next, plan the practical details, such as the location, menu, and logistics. It's important to involve local institutions and promote the event through adequate communication channels. Collaboration with other organizations can help ensure the success of the initiative and reach a wider audience.

### Useful Tools
To plan and organize a solidarity event, you can use tools like the [budget calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate costs and the [event calendar](nav:calendar) to plan dates. These tools can help you manage the organization effectively and ensure everything runs smoothly. The active participation of the community and the support of local institutions will be fundamental to the success of the initiative.

### Final CTA
If you want to learn more about organizing a solidarity event or participating in similar initiatives, visit our events section and discover how you can make a difference in your community. Your participation is crucial to promoting solidarity and mutual support.

item.title: Hunger strike in Varese: Timoc's fight for contested land
item.description: Timoc, 70, protests outside the Carabinieri barracks to access documents blocked in his home.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Timoc protests to access blocked documents
- Legal dispute over a plot of land for six years
- Precautionary measure prevents access to the house

## Key facts
- **What**: Hunger strike to access legal documents
- When: For several months
- Where: Via Saffi, Varese
- Who: Timoc, Romanian farmer and beekeeper
- Amount: Not specified

Timoc, 70 years old, has started a hunger strike in front of the Carabinieri barracks in Varese. The man, of Romanian origin and in Italy since 2000, is a farmer and beekeeper. His protest concerns access to documents kept in his home, blocked due to a precautionary measure. Timoc is subject to a precautionary measure and wears an electronic bracelet on his ankle following a two-year sentence for mistreating his ex-wife and three children. The presence of his ex-spouse in the house prevents him from re-entering to retrieve the papers, which he claims are fundamental to defending his rights over a disputed plot of land on Via Monte Generoso. The legal dispute concerns a plot of land and has lasted six years.

### The protest routine
Timoc patrols the area in front of the barracks during the day, but in the evening he is forced to return to his field, where he maintains his link with the agricultural life that has always characterized his years in Italy. His presence on Via Saffi is not continuous for 24 hours, but his determination is evident. He has already tried other avenues, even going to Rome to plead his case. His protest in front of the command on Via Saffi continues, an act of solitude but also of stubbornly demanding attention to a case that, for him, has become the focus of an entire existence.

### Practical implications
Timoc's situation highlights a difficult short circuit between the needs of justice and private urgencies. While the man claims the right to defend his patrimony, the restrictions imposed by the court seal off the places from which he has been removed. His protest is an example of how legal cases can deeply influence people's daily lives, especially when it comes to fundamental rights such as access to one's home and documents.

### Timoc's life
Timoc lives for his three twins, for whom he says he has always done everything possible. Despite the conviction, he continues to firmly deny all charges. His agricultural life, between caring for bees and chickens, is the only dimension he still fully feels his own. His determination to fight for his rights is an example of resilience and perseverance. ### The Legal Challenges
The legal dispute involving Timoc is complex and long-standing. The precautionary measure that prevents him from accessing his home was imposed following a conviction for abuse. This situation has created a short circuit between the needs of justice and private urgencies. Timoc claims that the documents blocked in the house are fundamental to defending his rights over a contested piece of land. His legal battle is a fight for justice and the defense of his own heritage.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
Timoc's case raises important issues for cross-border workers who work in Ticino. Legal disputes can have a significant impact on daily life, especially when it comes to access to documents and property. It is essential to know one's rights and legal procedures to defend them. Cross-border workers must be aware of precautionary measures and restrictions that can be imposed, and how these can influence their daily lives.

### Legal Procedures
For cross-border workers who find themselves in similar situations, it is important to follow legal procedures correctly. This includes filing complaints, gathering evidence, and consulting a lawyer specialized in civil and criminal law. It is also important to keep accurate documentation of all communications and legal actions taken. Cross-border workers must be proactive in defending their rights and seeking legal support when necessary.

### Available Resources
There are resources and support available for cross-border workers facing legal disputes. Organizations such as the External Relations Office of the Canton of Ticino can provide information and assistance. Additionally, cross-border workers can turn to specialized legal consultants who can help navigate the complexities of the Swiss and Italian legal systems. It is important to utilize these resources to ensure that one's rights are protected and that legal procedures are followed correctly.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do if you are involved in a legal dispute
If you are involved in a legal dispute similar to Timoc's, there are several concrete steps you can take. First and foremost, it is crucial to gather all relevant evidence and documents. This includes contracts, communications, and any other documentation that may support your legal position. Next, it is important to consult a lawyer specializing in civil and criminal law. A lawyer can provide legal advice and represent you in court.

### Legal deadlines
It is important to be aware of legal deadlines and procedures. Make sure to file all complaints and documents within the specified timeframes. Keep an accurate record of all communications and legal actions taken. This will help you defend your rights and ensure that legal procedures are followed correctly.

### Using available tools
There are tools and resources available to help cross-border workers navigate the complexities of the legal system. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help you understand the financial implications of legal disputes. Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the Frontaliere Ticino website to obtain information and support.

### Conclusion
Timoc's case is an example of how legal disputes can have a significant impact on daily life. It is important to know your rights and legal procedures to defend them. Cross-border workers must be proactive in seeking legal support and utilizing available resources to ensure their rights are protected. Use the tools and resources available to navigate the complexities of the legal system and defend your rights.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/sciopero-della-fame-davanti-alla-caserma-la-battaglia-di-timoc-per-un-terreno-conteso-da-sei-anni/2561185/)*

item.title: Sustainable fashion and stories of rebirth in Varese
item.description: Students and oncological women united in a sustainable fashion show in Varese, with recycled clothes and messages of resilience
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Sustainable fashion show in Varese with students and oncological women
- Recycled clothes and messages of resilience
- Event as part of EcoRun 2026

## Key facts
- **What**: Sustainable fashion show
- When: April 25, 2026
- Where: Varese, Piazza Monte Grappa
- Who: ISIS Newton students and women from the 'Le 0048' group
- Amount: Not yet specified

At EcoRun 2026 in Varese, a sustainable fashion show brought together students and oncological women. The event, titled 'Fashion and Sustainability,' took place on Saturday, April 25, in Piazza Monte Grappa, with an exhibition at the Torre Civica. The protagonists of the initiative were the students of the evening courses in Clothing Operator and Made in Italy at ISIS Newton, alongside the women of the group 'Le 0048 – The strength of oncological women.'

The fashion show represented the culmination of a journey built around three symbolic actions: destroy, assemble, and recreate. The outfits presented on the runway are the result of a recovery and regeneration process of existing clothes, transformed into new creations through recycling and enhancing materials. A clear message: it is possible to give new life to what seemed destined for discard.

### A message of resilience
Sustainability, as emerged from the speeches and the introductory text read during the event, is not only environmental. It is also – and above all – human. The women of Le 0048 brought an intense testimony, made of personal experiences marked by oncological disease, but also of strength, resilience, and ability to rebirth. 'We are not here just to see clothes, but to testify to a change,' was emphasized. A change that also passes through daily choices and awareness of the value of life.

The shared stories offered the young people present a direct and unfiltered reflection, inviting them not to take anything for granted and to recognize what truly matters. The initiative was born from the desire to bring together two worlds: that of women who face the disease with determination every day and that of young people who are building their future. A meeting that turned into a mutual exchange, where fragility became strength and creativity became a vehicle of meaning.

Alongside the students and Le 0048, the school principal Daniele Marzagalli and the teachers involved in the project participated in the event, as well as institutional representatives including the mayor of Varese Davide Galimberti and councilors Romana Dell’Erba, Emanuele Monti, and Michele Di Toro. 'Recycling means understanding that nothing is ever truly finished' was remembered during the evening. A concept that goes beyond materials and extends to people, experiences, and relationships. Thus, the runway became a symbol: not only of style, but of rebirth and solidarity. The 'Fashion and Sustainability' initiative in Varese was a concrete example of how fashion can become a vehicle for important messages. The students of ISIS Newton worked hard to transform existing clothes into new creations, demonstrating that recycling can be both creative and sustainable. This project not only showcased the students' skills but also gave them the opportunity to reflect on themes such as resilience and the value of life.

The women of the group 'Le 0048' brought a powerful testimony, showing how oncological disease can be faced with courage and determination. Their stories deeply touched the audience, inviting everyone to reflect on the meaning of life and the importance of living every moment with awareness. The fashion show was a moment of sharing and mutual growth, where fragility was transformed into strength and creativity into a message of hope.

### An educational and social project
The 'Fashion and Sustainability' project was born with the aim of creating an authentic and deep dialogue between different generations and experiences. The students had the opportunity to work closely with the women of the group 'Le 0048', learning not only recycling and regeneration techniques but also fundamental values such as resilience and solidarity. This exchange allowed the young people to understand the importance of supporting those who face difficulties, and the women to find a new purpose and energy through sharing their experiences.

The event also had a significant impact on the local community. The presence of institutional representatives, including the mayor of Varese Davide Galimberti, underscored the importance of initiatives that promote sustainability and solidarity. The fashion show was an opportunity to reflect on how small daily actions can make a difference, both for the environment and for society.

### Practical implications
For cross-border workers who live and work between Italy and Switzerland, initiatives like this can offer important food for thought. Sustainable fashion and recycling are not just environmental issues but also social ones. Promoting sustainability also means supporting local communities and creating opportunities for growth and sharing. This type of event can inspire new projects and collaborations, making society more cohesive and aware.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). If you are interested in participating in similar initiatives or learning more about sustainable fashion, you can visit the ISIS Newton website or contact the 'Le 0048' group for more information. Additionally, you can explore volunteering opportunities and collaborations with local associations that promote sustainability and support for people in need.

For cross-border workers who wish to delve deeper into sustainability and ethical fashion, you can participate in specific workshops and courses. These events offer not only practical knowledge but also the opportunity to connect with a community of like-minded individuals.

### Concrete Steps
1. **Inform yourself**: Visit the ISIS Newton website to discover upcoming events and initiatives.
2. Participate: Contact the 'Le 0048' group to join sustainable fashion projects.
3. Collaborate: Look for volunteering and collaboration opportunities with local associations.
4. Explore: Participate in workshops and courses on sustainable fashion and recycling.

### Useful Tools
To calculate the environmental impact of your fashion choices, you can use the [environmental impact calculator](nav:calculator). This tool will help you better understand how your daily choices can make a difference for the environment.

If you wish to delve deeper into the themes of sustainable fashion and resilience, visit the [sustainable fashion](nav:cost-of-living) section of our website for more information and useful resources.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/moda-sostenibile-e-storie-di-rinascita-a-varese-la-sfilata-che-unisce-studenti-e-donne-delle-0048/2561581/)*

item.title: Domus San Donato: safe autonomy for the elderly
item.description: New protected apartments for the elderly at Domus San Donato, built in the former convent. Freedom and assisted services
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New protected apartments for the elderly at Domus San Donato
- 12 apartments created in the former Sisters' building
- Autonomy and assisted services for the elderly

## Key facts
- **What**: New protected apartments for the elderly
- When: 30 April 2026
- Where: Domus San Donato, Ticino
- Who: Elderly institute
- Amount: 12 protected apartments

The Domus San Donato elderly institute has started the construction of 12 new protected apartments, created in the former Sisters' building. This project represents a new opportunity for the elderly, offering autonomy and security through assisted services. The apartments have been designed to ensure freedom and comfort, while maintaining a high level of care.

### An innovative project
The Domus San Donato project fits into a context of growing attention to the needs of the elderly population. The creation of protected apartments is a significant step towards greater autonomy for the elderly, who will benefit from safe spaces and personalized services. The initiative has been welcomed with enthusiasm by the local community, which sees in this project an example of innovation and attention to social needs.

The protected apartments at Domus San Donato have been designed to offer a balance between independence and assistance. The elderly will be able to enjoy private spaces and shared services, such as common areas and green spaces. The presence of qualified staff ensures constant support, guaranteeing safety and well-being.
