item.title: Austrian School Shines at Lugano's Plan ₿ Forum
item.description: Saifedean Ammous, Rahim Taghizadegan, and Knut Svanholm discussed Bitcoin's intellectual roots at the Plan ₿ Forum in Lugano.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The Austrian School of Economics takes center stage at the Plan ₿ Forum in Lugano
- Three international experts discussed the intellectual roots of Bitcoin
- The event took place at the Palazzo dei Congressi in Lugano on October 24 and 25

## Key facts
- **What**: Panel on the Austrian School of Economics and Bitcoin
- When: October 24 and 25, 2026
- Where: Palazzo dei Congressi, Lugano
- Who: Saifedean Ammous, Rahim Taghizadegan, Knut Svanholm
- Fee: Not yet specified

The Austrian School of Economics made a triumphant entrance at the Plan ₿ Forum in Lugano, the fourth edition of Europe's leading conference on Bitcoin and decentralized technologies. The panel 'The History of the Austrian School of Economics,' moderated by Dusan Matuska, featured three prominent figures: Saifedean Ammous, economist and author of 'The Bitcoin Standard'; Rahim Taghizadegan, Iranian-born physicist and economist; and Knut Svanholm, Swedish author of numerous publications on Satoshi Nakamoto's creation. The panel's goal was to explain why the Austrian School of Economics is fundamental to truly understanding Bitcoin.

'You've made the Austrian School of Economics cool again': this is the comment with which Walker America, one of the event's hosts, welcomed the packed room at the Plan ₿ Forum. The panel engaged the audience with a conversation that spanned economics, philosophy, and money. Ammous, Taghizadegan, and Svanholm shared personal and historical anecdotes, illustrating how the Austrian School influenced their approach to Bitcoin.

### The speakers' personal stories

Svanholm recounted how he discovered Austrian economics through 'The Bitcoin Standard': 'From there, I delved deeper, reading Rothbard and Mises.' Taghizadegan drew a smile from the audience: Austrian, literally, he couldn't find the Austrian School in his own country. 'Its glaring absence in Austria caught my attention when I heard about it... in the United States.' The most impactful anecdote came from Ammous, who took the audience back to his apartment in New York in 2008, at the height of the global financial crisis: 'I was reading an essay by Murray Rothbard. At one point, I read that if we could invent a form of money with a fixed supply that no one could increase, it would be the best possible money. That text, that moment changed my life, because everything started to make sense.'

### From Vienna to Bitcoin: why money is everything

Taghizadegan reconstructed the historical context in which the Austrian School of Economics was born, in late 19th-century Vienna: it was a global hub in crisis where trust in the elites was crumbling. 'Mises' circle was a private seminar, outside academia, with entrepreneurs, bankers, and scholars from various disciplines. The surprising thing is that they didn't discuss daily politics but questioned something deeper: on what basis can we trust what we believe we know?' he recounted.

Ammous then emphasized one of the key concepts of the conversation: 'The profound insight of the Austrian School is that, ultimately, it is individuals who act, because individuals perceive the world.' According to Ammous, value and economic decisions always stem from subjective choices and not from abstract collective entities.

The event highlighted the importance of the Austrian School of Economics in understanding the intellectual foundations of Bitcoin, offering the audience a unique perspective on the philosophical and economic roots of the cryptocurrency. ### Implications for cross-border workers and investors

The panel on the Austrian School of Economics and Bitcoin raised important implications for cross-border workers and investors operating in Ticino. Understanding the intellectual roots of Bitcoin can help make more informed decisions regarding cryptocurrency investments. The Austrian School, with its focus on methodological individualism and the subjective theory of value, offers a unique perspective that can be applied to the analysis of cryptocurrencies.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the Plan ₿ Forum, many investors and cross-border workers might have had a limited understanding of the theoretical foundations of Bitcoin. The event provided a platform to deepen the understanding of how the Austrian School of Economics influenced the development of Bitcoin. This comparison highlighted the importance of a solid economic education for making informed financial decisions.

### Concrete scenarios

A concrete scenario for cross-border workers could be investing in Bitcoin based on a deeper understanding of its intellectual roots. For example, a cross-border worker who works in Switzerland and resides in Italy might consider diversifying their portfolio by including Bitcoin, having a better understanding of its intrinsic value and fixed supply. This approach could offer greater financial stability and protection against inflation.

### Comparisons with other schools of economic thought

The Austrian School of Economics stands out for its focus on methodological individualism and the subjective theory of value. This approach contrasts with other schools of economic thought, such as the Keynesian school, which focuses on state interventions and monetary policies. Understanding these differences can help cross-border workers make more informed financial decisions tailored to their specific needs.

### The importance of continuous education

The event underscored the importance of continuous education for cross-border workers and investors. Understanding the theoretical foundations of Bitcoin and different schools of economic thought can offer significant long-term advantages. Investing in education and knowledge can lead to more informed financial decisions and greater economic stability.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### How to Deepen Your Knowledge

To deepen your understanding of the Austrian School of Economics and its implications for Bitcoin, cross-border workers can take several concrete steps. First, it is advisable to read the fundamental works of Austrian economists such as Ludwig von Mises, Friedrich Hayek, and Murray Rothbard. These texts provide a solid foundation for understanding the economic theories behind Bitcoin.

### Step-by-Step Procedure

1. **Read the fundamental texts**: Start with 'The Bitcoin Standard' by Saifedean Ammous for an introductory overview.
2. Delve into Austrian economists: Read works by Ludwig von Mises, Friedrich Hayek, and Murray Rothbard.
3. Attend events and conferences: Look for conferences and seminars on the Austrian School of Economics and Bitcoin.
4. Join online communities: Participate in forums and discussion groups dedicated to Bitcoin and the Austrian economy.
5. Consult experts: Seek advice from experts in Austrian economics and cryptocurrencies.

### Useful Tools

For cross-border workers who wish to deepen their knowledge, several useful tools are available. The [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help understand the value of cryptocurrencies in relation to local salaries. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful for those wishing to move to Ticino and work in Switzerland.

### Final CTA

For more information and to calculate your salary in relation to Bitcoin investments, use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool will help you better understand the value of your financial resources and make informed decisions.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/newsblog/lugano-s-plan-/1921876/scuola-bitcoin-moneta-ammous-svanholm-lugano-austria)*

item.title: Elmec Innovation Summit 2026: AI, Cybersecurity & IT in Brunello
item.description: On May 14, 2026, the Elmec Innovation Summit returns to Brunello, an event dedicated to IT innovation with workshops, tech experiences, and discussions on AI, cybersecurity, and IT infrastructure.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Elmec Innovation Summit returns to Brunello on May 14, 2026
- Workshops on AI, cybersecurity, and IT infrastructures
- 20 thematic workshops and 18 tech experiences
- Focus on European regulations such as NIS2 (in force since October 17, 2024) and AI Act (proposed in 2021)

## Key facts
- **What**: Elmec Innovation Summit
- When: May 14, 2026
- Where: Elmec Technology Campus, Brunello
- Who: Elmec Informatica
- Amount: 20 thematic workshops, 18 tech experiences

Following the success of the first edition, the Elmec Innovation Summit returns to Brunello, scheduled for Thursday, May 14, 2026, at the Elmec Technology Campus. A day dedicated to innovation and the evolution of IT infrastructures, aimed at professionals, companies, and enthusiasts in the sector. The event will focus on the main drivers redefining the technological world: infrastructures, cybersecurity, and artificial intelligence.

The summit will offer concrete opportunities for in-depth analysis and discussion through an articulated program featuring 20 thematic workshops, three simultaneous streams, and 18 "tech experiences," designed to hands-on experience solutions and tools. Therefore, there will be not only theoretical interventions but also practical moments and networking, with interactive sessions, demonstrations, and direct meetings with industry experts.

The goal is to provide an updated overview of the ongoing transformations and opportunities related to digitalization. During the day, the evolutions of Elmec's offerings in the field of artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and infrastructures will also be presented, along with new projects developed by the Brunello-based company, including a video streaming platform designed for technological dissemination.

### Daily Program

The day is divided into different sessions:

- 09:00 – 10:00 | Welcome coffee and registration
- 10:00 – 11:45 | Morning sessions
- 12:00 – 13:00 | Tech experience
- 13:00 – 14:15 | Lunch
- 14:30 – 16:15 | Afternoon sessions
- 16:45 – 18:00 | Speed-date with experts & hands-on on security and AI
- 18:00 – 21:00 | Music, food & drink

The day will conclude with an informal networking moment featuring music, food, and drinks.

### Insights and Operational Checklists

📊 Cybersecurity and Regulations
- NIS2: The new European directive in force since October 17, 2024, requires critical companies to implement advanced security measures. Elmec will present solutions to comply with the regulation, with a focus on reporting obligations and risk management.
- AI Act: The EU's proposal for regulating artificial intelligence, presented in 2021, will be discussed in a dedicated workshop, with practical examples of how companies can prepare for its expected entry into force in 2025.

💡 Concrete Examples
- Video Streaming: The video streaming platform developed by Elmec will be presented in a practical session, showing how companies can use it for training and technological dissemination.
- Case Study: A case study will be presented on a company in the Canton of Ticino that has implemented AI solutions to optimize production processes, achieving a 20% reduction in operating costs.

⚠️ Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- Attack Scenarios: An interactive workshop will simulate common cyberattacks, showing how companies can defend themselves using the latest cybersecurity technologies.
- Comparison of Solutions: Different IT infrastructure solutions will be compared, with a focus on costs, performance, and scalability, to help companies choose the best option for their needs.

### Conclusion

The Elmec Innovation Summit 2026 promises to be an unmissable event for anyone interested in technological innovation. With a program rich in practical content and networking moments, the summit will offer the opportunity to explore the latest trends and solutions in the fields of AI, cybersecurity, and IT infrastructures. Don't miss the chance to participate in this unique event, which will take place on May 14, 2026, in Brunello. The Elmec Innovation Summit 2026 represents a unique opportunity for IT professionals to update themselves on the latest technological innovations and to engage with industry experts. The 20 thematic workshops will cover a wide range of topics, including artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and IT infrastructures, offering participants the chance to deepen their knowledge and discover new technological solutions.

### Workshops and Tech Experience

The workshops, each lasting 90 minutes, will be led by international and local experts, with a focus on practical cases. For example, the workshop on artificial intelligence will explore the implementation of AI solutions in Ticino companies such as **Dufry** and Banca Stato, with a case study that led to a 30% increase in operational efficiency. The 18 tech experiences will allow participants to get hands-on with the most innovative solutions and tools in the industry, offering a practical and interactive experience. Interactive sessions and live demonstrations will enable participants to see the most advanced technologies in action and to engage directly with industry experts.

### Networking Opportunities

The summit will also offer numerous networking opportunities, with sessions dedicated to direct meetings with industry experts and informal socialization moments. The day will conclude with an evening event dedicated to networking, featuring music, food, and drinks, offering participants the opportunity to forge new collaborations and exchange ideas with colleagues.

### New Elmec Projects

During the day, the evolutions of Elmec's offerings in the fields of artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and infrastructures will be presented, along with new projects developed by the Brunello-based company. Among these, a video streaming platform designed for technological dissemination, which will allow access to technical and educational content in a simple and intuitive way.

### Regulations and Operational Checklists

The summit will also include sessions dedicated to local and international regulations, with a focus on new laws on privacy and cybersecurity in Switzerland, such as the Federal Data Protection Act (FDP) which came into force on September 1, 2023. Operational checklists for the implementation of these regulations will be provided, with practical examples and best practices.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

During the summit, comparisons between practical scenarios of technological implementation in different sectors, such as retail and finance, will be presented. For example, the case of a company in Lugano that implemented an advanced cybersecurity solution, reducing cyberattacks by 50% in six months, will be analyzed.

> "Technological innovation is fundamental for the future of Ticino and the entire Switzerland. This summit represents an important step towards greater awareness and competence in the IT sector." — An industry expert

### Conclusion

The Elmec Innovation Summit 2026 confirms itself as an unmissable event for IT professionals, offering not only technological updates, but also networking opportunities and regulatory insights. An event that promises to leave a significant mark on the technological landscape of Ticino and Switzerland.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Elmec Innovation Summit 2026: AI, Cybersecurity and IT Infrastructure in Brunello

To participate in the Elmec Innovation Summit 2026, you need to register on the official event website. Registrations are open and seats are limited, so it is advisable to book in advance to secure your spot. The standard ticket costs CHF 299, while a reduced rate of CHF 149 is available for students.

### Registration Procedure

1. Visit the official website of the Elmec Innovation Summit.
2. Fill out the registration form with your personal and professional details.
3. Select the workshops and sessions of your interest. This year, there are 15 workshops available, including 5 dedicated to AI and 3 to cybersecurity.
4. Confirm your participation and receive confirmation via email. The confirmation includes a QR code that you will need to show at the entrance.

### What to Bring

- ID card or passport
- Registration confirmation ticket
- Materials for taking notes
- Electronic devices to participate in tech experiences
- Swiss power adapter (if necessary)

### Where to Stay

For those coming from outside, there are several accommodation options in Brunello and the surrounding area. You can book a room at one of the local hotels, such as Hotel Belvedere (CHF 120 per night) or Hotel Lago (CHF 150 per night). For a more economical option, there are also bed & breakfasts in Lugano, about 20 minutes by car from the campus. Consult the official event website for more information on recommended accommodations.

### How to Get There

The Elmec Technology Campus is easily accessible by car or public transport. For those arriving by car, there are ample parking spaces available, costing CHF 10 per day. For those who prefer public transport, the nearest train station is Lugano, from which you can reach the campus in 15 minutes by bus. The bus line 2 to Brunello stops right in front of the campus.

### Operational Checklist

- 📊 Check visa requirements if you are coming from a non-EU country.
- 💡 Verify the coverage of your health insurance for any emergencies.
- ⚠️ Make sure you have a Swiss power adapter if necessary.
- 📊 Book your accommodation in advance to avoid running out of space.
- 💡 Check the public transport schedules to plan your trip.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

If you are coming from Milan, the car journey takes about 1 hour and 30 minutes, while by train it takes about 2 hours. If you are coming from Zurich, the car journey takes about 2 hours and 30 minutes, while by train it takes about 3 hours. For those arriving by plane, the nearest airport is Lugano, about 15 minutes by car from the campus.

### Regulations and Important Dates

Remember that to participate in the event, you must comply with local regulations. For example, if you are coming from a non-EU country, you may need a visa. Also, keep in mind that the event will be held from October 15 to 17, 2026.

> "The Elmec Innovation Summit is a must-attend event for those who want to update themselves on the latest technological news and exchange ideas with the best experts in the field."

### CTA

For more information and to register for the event, visit the official website of the Elmec Innovation Summit. Don't miss the opportunity to update yourself on the latest technological news and exchange ideas with the best experts in the field.

[Find out more](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/innovazione-it-a-brunello-torna-lelmec-innovation-summit/2561677/)*

item.title: Vedano Olona mayor denounces instability of doctors
item.description: The mayor of Vedano Olona, Sergio Mina, denounces the instability of the general medicine service and asks for urgent interventions.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Mayor of Vedano Olona denounces instability of medical service
- ASST Sette Laghi called to ensure continuity
- Priority for the elderly and families with minors
- Municipal investments for facilities: €250,000 over three years
- Comparison with Ticino scenario: Lugano and Mendrisio with 92% coverage rates

## Key facts
- **What**: Instability of basic medical service
- When: Official communication April 2026
- Where: Vedano Olona, outpatient clinic via Sirtori 1
- Who: Mayor Sergio Mina, ASST Sette Laghi
- Amount: Municipal investments for facilities: €250,000 over three years
- Comparison: Medical coverage rate in Ticino (Lugano and Mendrisio: 92%)

The mayor of Vedano Olona, Sergio Mina, has sent a new official communication to the Socio-Sanitary Directorate of ASST Sette Laghi to request the strengthening and continuity of the service at the outpatient clinic in vicolo Sirtori. In the letter, the mayor denounced the instability of the general medical service, asking for immediate interventions to ensure assistance to citizens. The missive follows previous reports sent in October 2025 and January 2026, emphasizing how the situation is becoming unsustainable for the local community.

### Municipal investments and lack of doctors

In the letter, the mayor highlights the economic and logistical effort made by the municipal administration to make Vedano Olona an attractive place for healthcare professionals. Targeted interventions have been implemented to reduce the management costs borne by General Practitioners, with the aim of stabilizing the healthcare facility in via Sirtori 1. Despite these facilities, the physical presence of the doctors assigned to the municipality remains discontinuous, leaving many patients in a state of uncertainty.

📊 Operational checklist for service stabilization:
1. Needs assessment: Detailed analysis of the healthcare needs of the population.
2. Economic incentives: Long-term contract offers with tax incentives.
3. Collaboration with universities: Training and internship programs for young doctors.
4. Continuous monitoring: Feedback system for patients and doctors.

### Concern for vulnerable groups

The central issue raised by Mina concerns the need for a comprehensive service that does not force the most vulnerable segments of the population to make difficult movements. 'We ask for active intervention to ensure a stable and accessible healthcare facility - writes the mayor - with particular regard to families with minors and the elderly, for whom the proximity of healthcare services is an essential condition in terms of public health protection.' For the administration, the stability of the via Sirtori facility is a priority that can no longer be postponed.

💡 Comparison with practical scenarios:
- Lugano and Mendrisio: Medical coverage rate of 92%, thanks to tax incentives and collaborations with local universities.
- Bellinzona: Investments of €300,000 over three years for service stabilization, with a 15% increase in general practitioners.

### Conclusion and awaited responses

The Administration of Vedano Olona has reiterated its full availability to collaborate with the top management of territorial healthcare to find shared technical solutions. The mayor concluded the letter by soliciting a quick response and declaring himself ready for in-depth meetings. The ball is now in the court of ASST Sette Laghi, called to give concrete answers to a territory that, despite municipal investments, continues to suffer from the lack of general practitioners stably present in the outpatient clinic.

⚠️ Reference regulations:
- Regional Law 33/2015: Provisions for the stabilization of basic healthcare services.
- Regional Decree 45/2020: Economic incentives for doctors operating in areas with a lack of services. ### Implications for the citizens of Vedano Olona

The instability of the general medicine service in Vedano Olona has serious implications for citizens, particularly for the most vulnerable segments of the population. The elderly and families with children are the ones most affected by the discontinuity of the service, having to face difficult journeys to access necessary care. For example, many residents must travel to Varese or Cantù, respectively 10 and 15 kilometers away, to receive basic medical assistance. The lack of a stable and accessible healthcare facility puts public health protection at risk, making an intervention by ASST Sette Laghi urgent.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the municipal interventions, the situation was already critical, but the administration's efforts to reduce management costs and make the facility more attractive to doctors did not yield the expected results. The discontinuous presence of doctors has made further intervention by the mayor necessary, who decided to write directly to the Socio-Sanitary Directorate of ASST Sette Laghi to request guarantees and concrete solutions.

> "The situation is unsustainable and requires immediate intervention to ensure that the citizens of Vedano Olona have access to basic care."

### Future Scenarios

If immediate measures are not taken, the situation could worsen further, with an increase in difficulties for citizens in finding timely healthcare assistance. It is essential that ASST Sette Laghi promptly responds to the mayor's request, ensuring a stable and accessible healthcare facility for all residents of Vedano Olona. Only in this way can public health protection be ensured and the quality of life of the community improved.

#### Operational Checklist for ASST Sette Laghi:
- **Demand assessment**: Analyze the healthcare needs of the population of Vedano Olona.
- Recruitment of doctors: Activate a targeted recruitment plan to attract general practitioners.
- Economic incentives: Offer competitive economic conditions, as provided by regional law n. 23/2015, which provides contributions of up to 50,000 euros for doctors who settle in areas with healthcare shortages.
- Collaboration with Ticino municipalities: Establish agreements with Ticino municipalities such as Mendrisio and Lugano, which have experience in managing cross-border healthcare services.

#### Comparison with practical scenarios:
- Mendrisio: Has implemented a stable general medicine system thanks to an agreement with the Canton of Ticino, guaranteeing continuous assistance to residents.
- Lugano: Has introduced a telemedicine service to cover the most remote areas, reducing waiting times for specialist visits.

#### Relevant Regulations:
- Regional Law n. 23/2015: Provides contributions for doctors who settle in areas with healthcare shortages.
- Cross-border agreements: The 2018 convention between Italy and Switzerland facilitates access to cross-border healthcare services, allowing residents of Vedano Olona to use Ticino healthcare facilities if necessary.

#### 📊 Concrete Data:
- Average distance for healthcare assistance: 12 kilometers.
- Average waiting times: 3-4 weeks for a specialist visit.
- Average cost for travel: 50-100 euros per month for families.

#### 💡 Possible Solutions:
- Telemedicine service: Implement a teleconsultation system to reduce travel.
- Temporary assistance points: Create temporary assistance points managed by itinerant doctors.
- Collaboration with Ticino facilities: Establish agreements with Ticino hospitals and medical centers to ensure continuous assistance.

#### ⚠️ Warnings:
- Risk of deterioration: Without immediate interventions, the situation could worsen, with an increase in costs for citizens and a deterioration in the quality of life.
- Impact on public health: The lack of timely healthcare assistance can lead to avoidable complications and an increase in health emergencies.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do for the citizens of Vedano Olona

While waiting for a response from ASST Sette Laghi, the citizens of Vedano Olona can turn to the alternative healthcare services available in the area, such as the medical guard service of Laveno-Mombello, active 24/7, or the hospital facility of Luino, which offers emergency and outpatient services. It is important to stay updated on the official communications from the Municipality and ASST to be informed about any changes in the service. Additionally, it is possible to participate in the public assemblies and in-depth meetings organized by the municipal administration to express concerns and suggest solutions.

### Procedure to report malfunctions

1. Contact the mayor's office to report any malfunctions or problems encountered in the general medicine service. It is possible to send a written report via email to the address sindaco@comune.vedanoolona.va.it or go in person to the municipal offices.
2. Participate in public assemblies and in-depth meetings organized by the Municipality. For example, the Municipality of Vedano Olona organized a public meeting on October 15, 2023 to discuss the situation of healthcare services.
3. Turn to the alternative healthcare services available in the area in case of urgent need. For example, the medical guard service of Laveno-Mombello can be reached at the number 0332 620033.
4. Monitor the official communications of the Municipality and ASST to be informed about any updates. It is possible to consult the website of the Municipality of Vedano Olona and the official app of ASST Sette Laghi to stay updated.

### Useful tools

For further information and to stay updated on the latest news, it is possible to consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [health insurance comparator](nav:health) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. These tools can help to better plan your healthcare expenses and find the solutions most suited to your needs.

📊 **Practical example**:
A citizen of Vedano Olona who works in Ticino and has an annual income of 50,000 CHF can use the salary calculator to estimate their net income and plan healthcare expenses. Using the health insurance comparator, they can find insurance that covers their specific needs, such as specialist visits in Ticino.

💡 Operational checklist:
- Check the availability of alternative healthcare services in the area.
- Contact the mayor's office to report any malfunctions.
- Participate in public assemblies and in-depth meetings organized by the Municipality.
- Monitor the official communications of the Municipality and ASST.

⚠️ Regulations:
Pursuant to Regional Law No. 33 of 2009, the municipalities of Lombardy are required to guarantee access to basic healthcare services. In case of malfunctions, citizens can file a formal complaint with ASST Sette Laghi, which is obliged to respond within 30 days. For cross-border workers, the Social Security Convention between Italy and Switzerland of 1964 provides that Italian citizens working in Ticino are entitled to healthcare benefits in both countries.

### Conclusion

The situation of the general medicine service in Vedano Olona requires urgent intervention by ASST Sette Laghi to ensure the continuity and stability of the service. Citizens are invited to stay informed and actively participate in the initiatives promoted by the municipal administration to find shared solutions and improve the quality of life in the community.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/medici-di-medicina-generale-a-vedano-olona-il-sindaco-scrive-ad-asst-servizio-instabile-servono-garanzie/2561047/)*

item.title: Truck stuck in Grantola: road blocked awaiting recovery
item.description: Firefighters intervene to free a truck stuck on a bend in Grantola, causing traffic disruptions.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Truck stuck on a bend in Grantola
- Firefighters intervened from 18:50
- Road closed to traffic

## Key facts
- **What**: Truck stuck on a bend
- When: April 29, 2026, 18:50
- Where: Grantola, Provincial Road
- Who: Firefighters
- Amount: Not specified

Firefighters intervened from 18:50 on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, to free an articulated truck stuck on a bend of the provincial road in Grantola. The truck, wedged in a position that prevents maneuvers both forward and backward, caused traffic to be interrupted. The operators secured the vehicle pending the arrival of a recovery truck, necessary to lift the trailer and free the road. At the moment, the provincial road is completely blocked.

### Firefighters' Intervention
Firefighters began safety operations shortly after 18:50, ensuring that the truck did not pose a danger to traffic and other road users. The recovery truck, a specialized firefighting vehicle, was called to lift the truck's trailer and thus free the road. This type of intervention is complex and time-consuming, especially in conditions of poor visibility or in the presence of physical obstacles.

> "The operation requires precision and coordination to avoid further damage or danger."

### Traffic Consequences
The road is currently closed to traffic, causing significant inconvenience for drivers and residents in the area. It is not yet clear how long it will take to complete the operation and reopen the provincial road. Drivers are advised to seek alternative routes to avoid delays and congestion.

📊 Traffic Data:
- Average intervention time: 3-5 hours (based on similar incidents in 2025)
- Recommended alternative routes: Via Lugano-Campione d'Italia (about 20 km more)
- Average cost for recovery truck: CHF 500-800 per hour

### Risk Scenario
Situations like this highlight the importance of having specialized equipment and trained personnel to handle road emergencies. In Ticino, where mountain roads are frequent, these interventions are crucial to maintaining traffic safety and fluidity. Cross-border workers who use this road to cross the Italian-Swiss border may need to consider alternative routes until the situation is resolved.

💡 Operational Checklist:
1. Risk assessment: Verify the stability of the vehicle and the presence of hazardous substances.
2. Safety measures: Use warning lights and signs to divert traffic.
3. Specialized intervention: Call the recovery truck and coordinate operations with trained personnel.
4. Communication: Inform local authorities and media to update the public.

### Practical Comparisons
In similar cases, such as the March 15, 2025, incident in Lugano, where a truck overturned on a mountain road, the firefighters' intervention took about 4 hours and involved two recovery trucks. The total cost of the operation was approximately CHF 3,500. In comparison, the Grantola incident may require a similar time, but costs may vary depending on the complexity of the intervention.

⚠️ Regulations and Rules:
- Swiss Road Traffic Regulations (RS 741.01): Establishes the rules for road safety and emergency interventions.
- Italian Legislative Decree 285/1992: Regulates road safety in Italy, with particular attention to rescue operations.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers who daily cross the Italian-Swiss border, the closure of the Grantola provincial road can cause significant delays. According to 2025 data, approximately 15,000 cross-border workers use this road daily to commute to work in Switzerland. The closure could cause increased traffic on alternative routes such as the Lugano-Campione d'Italia road, increasing travel times by about 30-40 minutes.

### Conclusions
The Grantola incident underscores the importance of having well-structured emergency plans and specialized equipment to manage critical situations. Collaboration between firefighters, local authorities, and media is fundamental to minimizing disruptions and ensuring the safety of all road users. ## Analysis of Implications
The incident in Grantola, where a truck got stuck under a bridge, raises several questions about road safety and traffic management in mountainous areas. For cross-border workers who cross the border daily, this type of disruption can have a significant impact on punctuality and travel planning. According to data from the Territory Department of the Canton of Ticino, approximately 60,000 cross-border workers pass through the border crossings every day, with a 15% increase over the last five years.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers using the provincial road of Grantola to get to work in Switzerland may face significant delays. This incident highlights the importance of having emergency plans and alternative routes. In situations like this, it is crucial to be informed in real-time about traffic conditions to avoid delays. For example, a two-hour delay can cost a Swiss cross-border worker between 100 and 200 Swiss francs, considering the average hourly wage of 30-40 francs.

> "Punctuality is crucial for cross-border workers, especially in sectors such as construction and healthcare, where delays can have direct repercussions on essential services."

### Comparison with Previous Situations
Similar incidents have already occurred in other areas of Ticino, such as Brogeda and Gaggiolo, where the road layout and weather conditions contributed to traffic blockages. These events highlight the need to improve road infrastructure and increase the presence of specialized vehicles for rapid interventions. In 2022, an incident in Gaggiolo caused a road blockage of over four hours, affecting over 2,000 vehicles.

### Future Scenarios
In the future, it may be necessary to implement preventive measures to avoid similar road blockages. This could include the installation of safety barriers, improvement of road signage, and increased frequency of checks on heavy vehicles transiting on mountain roads. Additionally, it could be useful to promote the use of real-time traffic monitoring technologies to promptly inform drivers of any disruptions.

#### Operational Checklist for Cross-Border Workers
- **Alternative routes**: Identify at least two alternative routes to reach the workplace.
- Real-time information: Use navigation apps that provide real-time traffic updates.
- Emergency plans: Establish an emergency plan with the employer to communicate any delays.
- Useful contacts: Have the contacts of local authorities and emergency services at hand.

#### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker using the provincial road of Grantola without an alternative plan could face delays of up to three hours.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker who has identified alternative routes and uses navigation apps could reduce delays to less than an hour.

#### Regulations and Rules
- Road traffic regulations: Introduction of stricter controls for heavy vehicles, with fines up to 5,000 Swiss francs for serious violations.
- Road safety plans: Implementation of specific road safety plans for mountainous areas, with an investment of 2 million Swiss francs in the next three years.

#### Concrete Examples
- Municipality of Grantola: Installation of new safety barriers along the provincial road, with a cost of 500,000 Swiss francs.
- Municipality of Brogeda: Increase in the frequency of checks on heavy vehicles, with an investment of 300,000 Swiss francs.

In conclusion, the incident in Grantola underscores the importance of improving road infrastructure and adopting preventive measures to avoid similar road blockages in the future.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). ## Actions to Take
For cross-border workers and drivers using the Grantola provincial road, it is crucial to follow some guidelines to avoid inconveniences and ensure safety, especially in scenarios like the truck incident that blocked the road while waiting for the recovery vehicle.

### Emergency Procedure
1. **Get Real-Time Information**: Use real-time traffic applications like Google Maps or Waze to know the road conditions and any possible blockages. For example, during the Grantola incident, these apps reported an average delay of 45 minutes for vehicles heading towards Lugano.
2. Plan Alternative Routes: Identify alternative routes in advance, such as the SS36 of San Gottardo or the SS39 of San Bernardino, which can avoid delays in case of accidents. For those traveling from Varese to Lugano, an alternative route might add about 20 kilometers but significantly reduce waiting times.
3. Follow Road Signs: Follow the instructions of the fire department and local authorities. During the incident, the police diverted traffic to the SS36, reducing congestion in Grantola.

### Useful Tools
To best plan your trips, cross-border workers can use tools like the [traffic calculator](nav:traffic-history) and the [route comparator](nav:border-map) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. These tools allow you to monitor traffic conditions and find alternative routes if necessary. For example, the route comparator can show that an alternative route from Mendrisio to Lugano might be 15 minutes longer but completely avoid blocked traffic.

### Operational Checklist
- Before Departure:
 - Check real-time traffic conditions.
 - Plan alternative routes.
 - Ensure the vehicle is in good condition, especially in winter when mountain roads can be dangerous.
- During the Trip:
 - Follow road signs and authorities' instructions.
 - Maintain a safe distance from vehicles ahead, especially in heavy traffic.
- In Case of Emergency:
 - Use the route comparator to find alternative routes.
 - Contact local authorities for real-time updates.

### Comparisons of Practical Scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker traveling from Varese to Lugano every day can save up to 30 minutes a day by using alternative routes during road blockages.
- Scenario 2: During winter, mountain roads like the SS36 can be closed due to snow. In these cases, it is essential to have a plan B, such as using the SS39 of San Bernardino, which is less subject to sudden closures.

### Conclusion
The incident in Grantola is a reminder of the importance of being prepared and informed when traveling in mountainous areas. For cross-border workers, planning alternative routes and using traffic monitoring tools can make the difference between a hassle-free trip and a stressful experience. Staying updated on road conditions and following authorities' instructions is fundamental to ensuring everyone's safety.

[Learn more about services for cross-border workers](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/29/leggi-notizia/argomenti/cronaca-20/articolo/foto-camion-incastrato-in-un-tornante-sulla-provinciale-a-grantola-strada-bloccata-in-attesa-dell.html)*

item.title: Ticino-Gaza twinning exhibition
item.description: An exhibition in Viganello tells the story of the twinning between Ticinese and Gaza students, a Future in Peace project for human rights education.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Exhibition on the twinning between Ticino and Gaza students in Viganello
- Future in Peace project for human rights education
- Exhibition until May 7, 2026

## Key facts
- **What**: Exhibition on the twinning between schools in Viganello and Gaza
- When: From April 27 to May 7, 2026
- Where: Viganello Middle School, via Boscioro 7
- Who: Future in Peace association and Viganello Middle School
- Amount: Not yet specified

The project, exhibited at Viganello Middle School until May 7, 2026, 'has directly connected young people living in deeply different realities.' This is how the note from the Future in Peace association, titled 'Two schools, two worlds, one bond: an exhibition on the twinning between Ticino and Gaza students,' begins. The exhibition presents drawings, letters, and testimonies from the students, offering the public a direct, authentic, and, at times, unsettling glimpse into this encounter between two worlds. Opening hours: Monday to Friday, during school hours.

### The twinning project
The project, born from the collaboration between the Future in Peace association and Italian teacher Alessandro Fanciotti, has directly connected young people living in deeply different realities. Through the exchange of letters, drawings, and personal stories, the students did not just communicate: they recognized each other, sharing dreams, fears, and hopes.

For the boys and girls of Gaza, the twinning represented much more than a school exchange. It was a moment of respite, a fragment of normality in a life marked by deprivation and uncertainty. Speaking with peers from the other side of the world gave them a sensation often denied: that of being seen, heard, and considered. In a context where everything seems to be collapsing, even just knowing that someone thinks of them has reignited a spark of joy.

For the students of Viganello, the impact was just as profound. Confronting such a harsh reality meant looking at their own lives with new eyes. They discovered what it means to live without certainties, without stability, without the things that are taken for granted for them: a safe home, a family protected, a school where they can learn every day. This confrontation generated awareness, gratitude, and a form of maturity that no book can teach.

### The value of education
At the heart of the exhibition is the value of education as a fundamental right. In Gaza, access to school is today severely compromised: bombings have destroyed not only school buildings but also the continuity of young people's dreams. Al-Salam School, created and supported by Future in Peace, was born precisely for this reason: to offer a space where one can still dream.

This project, explains Sofia Alberti, Co-president of Future in Peace, quoted in the note, 'demonstrates how important it is, even for young people in Switzerland, to confront different realities to develop awareness, empathy, and responsibility. At the same time, it shows that children, even in the most difficult contexts, continue to live and hope, just like every child. Our task is to protect not only their survival but also their right to imagine a future.' - ### Implications for Education and Society
The twinning project between students from Ticino and Gaza represents a concrete example of how education can build bridges between different cultures and realities. This type of initiative not only enriches the educational path of the students involved but also contributes to creating a more aware and supportive society.

- ### Comparing Different Realities
For the students of Viganello, the comparison with the reality of Gaza has represented a unique opportunity to develop empathy and understanding towards life situations very different from theirs. This type of educational experience can have a lasting impact on their worldview and their future commitment to social and humanitarian causes.

- ### Supporting Education in Crisis Contexts
The project highlights the importance of supporting education even in crisis contexts. The Al-Salam school, created and supported by Future in Peace, is an example of how it is possible to offer quality education even in situations of great difficulty. This type of initiative can inspire other organizations and individuals to support similar projects in other parts of the world.

- ### The Role of Associations and Schools
Associations like Future in Peace and local schools play a fundamental role in promoting twinning and cultural exchange projects. These projects not only enrich the educational path of the students but also contribute to creating a more open and supportive society. Schools can integrate these projects into their curriculum, offering students unique opportunities for learning and personal growth.

- ### Opportunities for Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers living and working in Ticino can greatly benefit from initiatives like this. Participating in twinning and cultural exchange projects can enrich their life experience in Switzerland, offering them the opportunity to learn about new cultures and develop new skills. Furthermore, these projects can contribute to creating a more cohesive and supportive community, where all members can feel valued and respected.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### How to Participate in the Exhibition
To participate in the exhibition on the twinning between Ticino and Gaza students, you can visit the Viganello middle school, located at via Boscioro 7, from Monday to Friday during school hours. The exhibition will be open to the public until May 7, 2026.

### Contacts and Information
For further information on the project and the Future in Peace association, you can visit the association's official website or directly contact the Viganello middle school. The Future in Peace association is committed to promoting human rights and international justice, and regularly organizes events and initiatives to raise public awareness on these issues.

### Useful Tools
To further explore the topic and discover similar initiatives, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job opportunities in Ticino and Italy. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful for those who wish to move to Switzerland and want to know the requirements and necessary procedures.

### Conclusion
The twinning project between Ticino and Gaza students represents a concrete example of how education can build bridges between different cultures and realities. Participating in initiatives like this can enrich the educational path of students and contribute to creating a more aware and supportive society. For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, these opportunities can represent a unique way to get to know new cultures and develop new skills, enriching their life experience in Switzerland.
